title: fix dim error in rope
body:
fix the following error when running _test_rotary():

"""
...
    x_rope = (x_rope * self.cos_cached[:x.shape[0]]) + (neg_half_x * self.sin_cached[:x.shape[0]])
RuntimeError: The size of tensor a (3) must match the size of tensor b (4) at non-singleton dimension 3
"""

step_plan.summary: Fixes the RoPE dimension mismatch by enforcing that the rotary dimension is even (pairwise rotation), ensuring caches and forward shapes are consistent, rounding attention’s computed `d_rope` to an even value, and updating `_test_rotary()` to reflect the corrected contract.

steps.operation: Enforce even rotary dimension `d` (raise clear error if odd) and document the constraint in-code
steps.reason: RoPE rotates features in pairs; the current implementation (including `_neg_half` and cache construction) implicitly assumes an even number of rotated channels. Rejecting odd `d` prevents silent cache/activation shape divergence that later triggers the (3 vs 4) runtime error.
steps.side_effects: Any existing callers passing an odd `d` will now fail fast with a descriptive exception; tests or downstream code may need to adjust `d` or rounding logic.

steps.operation: Make cache construction consistent with the enforced even `d` and add internal assertions to catch shape regressions
steps.reason: The observed mismatch comes from cache width becoming `2*ceil(d/2)` when `d` is odd. With `d` enforced even, `_build_cache` must produce `cos_cached/sin_cached` with last dimension exactly `d`, and should assert this invariant so future changes don’t reintroduce the bug.
steps.side_effects: Slight overhead from assertions (can be guarded/debug-only); cached tensors’ shapes become strictly tied to `d`, which may affect any external code that relied on previous (buggy) cache sizing.

steps.operation: Guard `forward()` against invalid shapes by validating input last-dim compatibility with `d` and cache width before applying the rotation
steps.reason: `forward()` is where the multiplication happens; adding explicit checks (e.g., `x.shape[-1] >= d` and `cos/sin` last-dim equals `d`) turns hard-to-debug PyTorch broadcast errors into actionable messages and ensures safe slicing of the RoPE portion.
steps.side_effects: May raise earlier, clearer errors for misconfigured models; minimal runtime cost for shape checks. If callers previously passed tensors with unexpected layout, they may need to fix tensor shapes.

steps.operation: Ensure `d_rope` computed from `rope_percentage` is always even (round/floor to nearest even and clamp to valid range) before constructing `RotaryPositionalEmbeddings`
steps.reason: Upstream, `int(self.d_k * rope_percentage)` can easily yield an odd number. Normalizing `d_rope` to an even value prevents invalid configurations in real attention usage, aligning with RoPE’s pairwise rotation requirement.
steps.side_effects: Model configs may get a slightly different effective `rope_percentage` due to rounding to even; needs to be reflected in any documentation or expectations. Should consider edge cases like very small `rope_percentage` producing `d_rope=0`.

steps.operation: Update `_test_rotary()` to either use an even RoPE dimension or assert that odd `d` is rejected with the expected exception
steps.reason: The current test instantiates `RotaryPositionalEmbeddings(3)` which is invalid under pairwise rotation. The test should validate the intended behavior: either (a) use a correct even dimension for functional testing, and/or (b) explicitly test that odd dimensions raise a clear error so regressions are caught.
steps.side_effects: Test behavior changes: it will no longer reproduce the original crash; instead it will validate the corrected contract (even `d`) and prevent future reintroduction of the mismatch.
